description_long: This object consists of two medium-sized, square wooden storage boxes placed side by side. Each box is constructed from planks of wood, with visible horizontal and vertical slats reinforcing the sides and top, giving a sturdy and rustic appearance. The wood is weathered, with a muted brown color and subtle grain patterns, suggesting outdoor or utilitarian use. The tops of the boxes are flat and appear to be removable, with no visible hinges or locks, indicating a simple design. There are no handles, labels, or decorative elements, emphasizing functionality over aesthetics. The boxes are likely used for storing tools, supplies, or other items in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or outdoor spaces.
description: A pair of square, weathered wooden storage boxes with flat, removable tops and reinforced slat construction. The design is utilitarian, with no handles or labels, suitable for garages or outdoor use.
description_view_0: Front view showing two square wooden boxes side by side, highlighting the flat tops and reinforced slat construction.
description_view_1: Side view displaying the depth of the boxes and the layered wooden slats, with one box partially obscured behind the other.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the backs of both boxes and the consistent slat pattern.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the depth and construction details, with the boxes aligned one behind the other.
